steps.title: Combine rank and crew size
steps.content: First-rate ships had 850 to 875 crew members, while lower-ranked ships had fewer. The Boyne had three decks but was not first-rate, so it was a lower-ranked three-deck ship with fewer than 850 crew members.

steps.title: Reject unsupported conclusions
steps.content: B exceeds the stated first-rate range. C reverses the ranking relationship between the Boyne and the Britannia. D is contradicted by the Boyne, which had 98 cannons but fewer than 850 crew members. At least one three-deck ship had fewer than 850 people. The answer is A.
